name: Channel-Keyed Pseudonyms
description: Deterministic per-channel pseudonym generation module (`static/js/pseudonyms.js`) using HKDF + HMAC-SHA256 via Web Crypto API. Produces unlinkable, deterministic pseudonyms from an Ed25519 private key and a channel identifier. No caching — callers are responsible for caching results keyed on channelId. Pure functions, no classes, no state. Uses `window.base64js` global for base64 encode/decode and `crypto.subtle` for all cryptographic operations.

types.name: Base64String
types.description: A string containing valid base64-encoded data. For pseudonyms generated by this module, this will be a 44-character base64 string representing 32 bytes of HMAC-SHA256 output.

types.name: PrivateKeyBase64
types.description: A base64-encoded Ed25519 secret key (64 bytes decoded). The TweetNaCl secret key format: first 32 bytes are the raw seed/key material, last 32 bytes are the public key. Only the first 32 bytes are used as HKDF input key material.

types.name: ChannelId
types.description: A non-empty string identifying a channel. UTF-8 encoded before being signed with HMAC-SHA256 to produce the pseudonym.

types.name: HkdfParams

fields.name: salt
fields.description: Always empty Uint8Array (new Uint8Array(0)). Empty salt per HKDF spec defaults to hash-length zero bytes.

fields.name: info
fields.description: UTF-8 encoding of the constant string 'hermes-pseudonym'. Used as the HKDF info parameter for domain separation.

types.description: Fixed HKDF parameters used internally for key derivation. Not exported — documented here for contract completeness.

types.name: DerivedHmacKeyParams
types.description: Fixed parameters for the derived HMAC key. Not exported — documented for contract completeness.

types.name: ModuleConstants

fields.name: IKM_LENGTH
fields.description: Number of bytes taken from the Ed25519 secret key as input key material: 32

types.description: Module-level constants defined at the top of pseudonyms.js for clarity and auditability.

functions.name: generatePseudonym
functions.description: Generates a deterministic, channel-specific pseudonym from an Ed25519 private key and a channel identifier. Uses HKDF to derive a per-purpose HMAC-SHA256 key from the first 32 bytes of the Ed25519 secret key, then signs the UTF-8-encoded channelId with that key. The result is a 44-character base64 string. Same inputs always produce the same output (deterministic). Different channelIds produce different pseudonyms (unlinkable across channels). Different private keys produce different pseudonyms for the same channel. This function is async because it uses crypto.subtle operations. NEVER logs private key material.

inputs.name: privateKeyBase64
inputs.description: Base64-encoded Ed25519 secret key (64 bytes when decoded). The TweetNaCl keypair secretKey in base64 format. First 32 bytes are used as HKDF input key material.

validators.error_message: privateKeyBase64 must be a non-empty string
privateKeyBase64 must decode to at least 32 bytes (Ed25519 secret key seed)

inputs.name: channelId
inputs.description: Non-empty string identifying the channel. UTF-8 encoded before HMAC signing. Different channelIds yield different pseudonyms for the same key.

validators.error_message: channelId must be a non-empty string

error_cases.name: missing_private_key
error_cases.condition: privateKeyBase64 is undefined, null, or empty string

error_data.message: privateKeyBase64 is required and must be a non-empty string

error_cases.name: private_key_too_short
error_cases.condition: Decoded privateKeyBase64 is less than 32 bytes

error_data.message: privateKeyBase64 must decode to at least 32 bytes

error_cases.name: invalid_base64_private_key
error_cases.condition: privateKeyBase64 is not valid base64 and base64js.toByteArray() throws

error_data.message: Failed to decode privateKeyBase64: invalid base64

error_cases.name: missing_channel_id
error_cases.condition: channelId is undefined, null, or empty string

error_data.message: channelId is required and must be a non-empty string

error_cases.name: webcrypto_unavailable
error_cases.condition: crypto.subtle is not available in the runtime environment

error_data.message: crypto.subtle is required but not available

error_cases.name: base64js_unavailable
error_cases.condition: window.base64js is not available in the runtime environment

error_data.message: window.base64js is required but not available

error_cases.name: hkdf_import_failure
error_cases.condition: crypto.subtle.importKey rejects (e.g., invalid key data for HKDF)

error_data.message: Failed to import key material as HKDF key

error_cases.name: key_derivation_failure
error_cases.condition: crypto.subtle.deriveKey rejects during HMAC key derivation

error_data.message: Failed to derive HMAC key from HKDF key
Failed to sign channelId with derived HMAC key

preconditions: privateKeyBase64 is a non-empty string containing valid base64 data
Decoded privateKeyBase64 is at least 32 bytes long (Ed25519 secret key format)
window.base64js global is available with toByteArray() and fromByteArray() methods
crypto.subtle global is available with importKey(), deriveKey(), and sign() methods
postconditions: Returns a 44-character base64 string (representing 32 bytes of HMAC-SHA256 output)
Return value is deterministic: identical inputs always produce identical output
Return value differs when channelId differs (unlinkability across channels)
Return value differs when privateKeyBase64 differs (unlinkability across identities)
No private key material is logged to console at any point during execution
The HKDF key is imported as non-extractable (extractable: false)

structured_side_effects.description: Pure computation — no side effects, no logging, no state mutation, no network calls. All crypto operations are performed in-memory via Web Crypto API.

functions.name: shortenPseudonym
functions.description: Returns the first `length` characters of a base64-encoded pseudonym string, useful for display purposes. Clamps the length to the range [0, pseudonymBase64.length] so it never throws on out-of-range length values. This is a synchronous, pure function.

inputs.name: pseudonymBase64
inputs.description: A base64-encoded pseudonym string, typically the 44-character output of generatePseudonym().

inputs.name: length
inputs.description: Number of leading characters to return. Defaults to 8. Clamped to [0, pseudonymBase64.length].

error_data.message: pseudonymBase64 is required and must be a string

error_cases.name: invalid_length_type
error_cases.condition: length is provided but is not a number or not an integer

preconditions: pseudonymBase64 is a string (may be empty)
length is an integer (or omitted, defaulting to 8)
postconditions: Return value length equals Math.min(Math.max(0, length), pseudonymBase64.length)
Return value is a substring starting at index 0 of pseudonymBase64
Return value contains only characters from the original pseudonymBase64 string
When called with default length on a 44-character pseudonym, returns an 8-character string
When length exceeds pseudonymBase64.length, returns the full pseudonymBase64 string
When length is 0 or negative, returns an empty string

structured_side_effects.description: Pure synchronous string operation — no side effects.

invariants: Module exports exactly two named functions: generatePseudonym and shortenPseudonym. No default export.
No private key material, secret key bytes, or HKDF-derived key material is ever logged to console (console.log, console.warn, console.error, or any other output channel)
generatePseudonym is deterministic: for any fixed (privateKeyBase64, channelId) pair, every invocation returns the identical Base64String
generatePseudonym provides channel unlinkability: for any fixed privateKeyBase64, generatePseudonym(key, channelA) !== generatePseudonym(key, channelB) when channelA !== channelB

generatePseudonym provides identity unlinkability: for any fixed channelId, generatePseudonym(keyA, channel) !== generatePseudonym(keyB, channel) when keyA !== keyB (different underlying key material)
The HKDF info parameter is always the UTF-8 encoding of the constant 'hermes-pseudonym' — never varies per call
The HKDF salt is always an empty Uint8Array (new Uint8Array(0)) — never varies per call
Only the first 32 bytes of the decoded private key are used as HKDF input key material
The HKDF key is imported with extractable=false to prevent key material extraction
The derived HMAC key is created with extractable=false and usages=['sign'] only
Module contains no classes — only pure exported functions
Module contains no internal caching or memoization — caching is the caller's responsibility
Module file is located at static/js/pseudonyms.js
All public (exported) functions have JSDoc comments
Module uses ES6 import/export syntax — no CommonJS require/module.exports
Constants INFO_STRING and SALT are defined at the top of the module file
requires: window.base64js global (base64-js library) providing toByteArray() and fromByteArray()
crypto.subtle global (Web Crypto API) providing importKey(), deriveKey(), and sign()
